name: KORU Medical Systems, Inc.
business: KORU Medical Systems develops, manufactures, and commercializes large-volume subcutaneous infusion systems, primarily the FREEDOM System, for delivery of subcutaneous drugs such as immunoglobulin to treat Primary Immunodeficiency Diseases (PIDD) and Chronic Inflammatory Demyelinating Polyneuropathy (CIDP). Revenues are derived from three sources: domestic core (US and Canada), international core, and pharma services and clinical trials. The company sells its products through distributors, specialty pharmacies, and home infusion providers.

exposures.sentence: KORU Medical develops, manufactures and commercializes innovative and patient-centric large volume subcutaneous infusion solutions primarily for the subcutaneous drug delivery market as governed by the United States Food and Drug Administration (the FDA ) quality and regulatory system and international standards for quality system management.

tamClaims.claim: At least 170 large-volume drugs (greater than 2ml) are in clinical development utilizing subcutaneous infusion, with approximately 20% greater than 10ml.
tamClaims.sentence: In our goal to expand into novel therapies outside of SCIg, we estimate that at least 170 large-volume drugs, greater than 2ml, are in clinical development utilizing subcutaneous infusion, with approximately 20% greater than 10ml.
